title: Pixelate cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: the coarser held-window replay setting should push the exported decoded-media pixelate spectrum materially harder than the lighter setting

expectedVideo: the decoded fixture should visibly quantise into larger blocks as pixel counts drop
motion should remain continuous while fine detail is intentionally reduced
expectedAudio: fixture audio should pick up the same held-window replay law heard on the osc case
the rendered mix should remain audible and controlled across the stronger setting
manualChecks: Does pixelate stay legible and usable on decoded media rather than looking like a broken upload?
Does the audio texture change feel like the same operator family as the visual blockiness, not like a generic bitcrusher?
